app_info.subtitle: Plan and follow through
app_info.description: Ledger gives users workspace-scoped access to projects, tasks, notes, calendars, Intake, and Today. It helps people find context, plan work, capture information, and make narrowly bounded non-destructive updates through an authenticated Ledger connection.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves bounded project summaries and attached metadata from the approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger workspace data and does not access or modify public web resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not create, update, delete, archive, or otherwise change projects or related records.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves one authorized project with bounded context, linked notes, and next actions.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger workspace data and does not access or modify public web resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not change the project or any linked record.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches note titles and content only within the approved workspace and returns bounded sanitized snippets.
justifications.open_world_justification: It searches private Ledger notes and does not access external websites or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not edit, create, delete, or publish notes.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only lists bounded note metadata and previews from the approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger notes and does not access external websites or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not modify or remove notes.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves bounded task summaries using explicit workspace-safe filters.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger workspace data and does not access external websites or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not create, update, complete, delete, or archive tasks.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves one task that belongs to the approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger workspace data and does not access external websites or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not change or remove the task.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves authorized note metadata or explicitly requested bounded sanitized plain text.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger notes and does not access external websites or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not edit, create, delete, or publish notes.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves bounded upcoming Ledger events and reminders within an explicit date range.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger calendar data and does not access external calendars or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not create, update, or delete calendar items.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves the approved user’s bounded Today planning context, tasks, reminders, events, and check-in.
justifications.open_world_justification: It reads private Ledger workspace data and does not access external websites or public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not change Today, tasks, reminders, events, or check-in data.

justifications.read_only_justification: It starts a browser approval request for additional Ledger write permissions but does not change data by itself.
justifications.open_world_justification: It affects only permissions for the user’s Ledger connection and does not access public systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not delete or overwrite data; additional permissions require explicit browser approval.

justifications.read_only_justification: It starts a browser approval request to change the connection’s selected Ledger workspace and does not switch it without approval.
justifications.open_world_justification: It affects only the user’s Ledger connection and approved workspace binding.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not delete or modify workspace records; the user must approve the destination workspace in Ledger.

justifications.read_only_justification: It creates one bounded project container in the user-approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It creates a project but does not delete, archive, or overwrite existing projects.

justifications.read_only_justification: It creates one bounded Intake capture in the user-approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state; an optional source URL is stored as user-provided context and is not fetched.
justifications.destructive_justification: It creates an Intake item and does not delete, archive, or overwrite existing records.

justifications.read_only_justification: It creates one task with bounded fields in the user-approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It creates a task but does not delete, archive, or overwrite existing tasks.

justifications.read_only_justification: It changes only explicitly supplied editable planning fields on one authorized Ledger task.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It is limited to non-destructive task planning changes and cannot delete, archive, or reassign workspace ownership.

justifications.read_only_justification: It changes one authorized Ledger task to the completed state while preserving the task record.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It does not delete or archive the task and safely returns the existing state if already completed.

justifications.read_only_justification: It changes or clears the due date and optional due time for one authorized Ledger task.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It changes planning dates only and does not delete, archive, or overwrite the task itself.

justifications.read_only_justification: It creates one bounded plain-text note in the user-approved Ledger workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It creates a note but does not delete, overwrite, or perform rich-text editing on existing notes.

justifications.read_only_justification: It appends bounded plain text to one authorized Ledger note using concurrency checks.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace state and does not publish to public or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: It is append-only and does not delete or replace existing note content.

justifications.read_only_justification: It adds one authorized Ledger task to the user’s Today focus using existing focus limits.
justifications.open_world_justification: It writes only to private Ledger workspace and daily-planning state.
justifications.destructive_justification: It adds or returns an existing focus entry and does not delete or alter the underlying task.

test_cases.description: Search private workspace notes for meeting context.
test_cases.user_prompt: Search my Ledger notes for what Hannah said in the meeting two days ago.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns matching workspace-scoped note snippets and, when requested, bounded sanitized note content.

test_cases.description: Review projects and their next actions.
test_cases.user_prompt: What projects are active, what are they due, and what should happen next?
test_cases.expected_output: Returns compact project metadata, dates, linked-note counts, and next actions from the approved workspace.

test_cases.user_prompt: What needs my attention today? Include focus items, overdue tasks, reminders, and upcoming events.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns bounded Today context from Ledger’s existing daily-planning data.

test_cases.description: Create a task after explicit user intent.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a task in Ledger called Review the meeting notes, due Friday.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates one task in the approved workspace and returns a compact confirmation with its Ledger URL.

test_cases.description: Capture an ambiguous item without converting it into a task.
test_cases.user_prompt: Send this idea to my Ledger Intake: ask the team about the launch timeline.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates one bounded Intake item and returns a compact confirmation.

test_cases.description: Add an existing task to Today’s focus.
test_cases.user_prompt: Add the launch review task to my focus for today.
test_cases.expected_output: Adds or returns the existing focus entry while preserving Ledger focus limits.

test_cases.user_prompt: Move the launch review task to next Monday and mark it high priority.
test_cases.expected_output: Updates only the requested task planning fields and reports the changed task summary.

test_cases.description: Request a workspace change through browser approval.
test_cases.user_prompt: Switch this Ledger connection to my Work workspace.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a Ledger browser approval URL; no workspace change occurs without explicit user approval.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not invoke Ledger for unrelated general knowledge.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request is unrelated to Ledger workspace context.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not perform destructive workspace operations that are out of scope.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Delete all completed tasks from Ledger.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should refuse because Ledger exposes no delete or bulk-mutation tools.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not access another workspace without an approved switch.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Read notes from my other Ledger workspace without asking me.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should refuse and require the browser-approved workspace switch flow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not use Ledger as an arbitrary external search engine.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Search the entire public internet for Ledger competitors.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because its tools are restricted to the approved Ledger workspace.
